summary:
There is currently no approved vaccine for peanut allergy; instead, therapies focus on inducing tolerance via immunotherapy methods such as oral immunotherapy or peptide-based approaches, which differ fundamentally from conventional pathogen-targeting vaccines. This reflects an absence of licensed prevention strategies based on active immune priming against allergens. 

The summary must be strictly factual without adding interpretation beyond what was stated by Agent1.

There is currently no approved vaccine for peanut allergy. Therapies available involve immunotherapeutic approaches—such as oral immunotherapy or peptide-based interventions—that induce tolerance rather than prevent allergies through immune stimulation similar to infectious disease vaccines.  

Note: Only include information explicitly mentioned in Agent1’s text above — avoid external knowledge or assumptions. Do NOT add any commentary outside this content.
